title: pyrus ussuriensis var. macrostipes
intro: Pyrus ussuriensis var. macrostipes, also known as the Manchurian pear, is a deciduous tree native to East Asia, characterized by its broad, rounded crown and white or pale yellow flowers. The bark and leaves of the Manchurian pear are used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving digestion, as well as possessing antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of Pyrus ussuriensis var. macrostipes are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal properties.
